description: rig hooks for Cursor (#304, generalized from #294's Codex adapter via scripts/host_adapters.py). Cursor's `preCompact` is documented as purely observational (cursor.com/docs/hooks) — it cannot inject preserved context into the compaction the way Claude Code's PreCompact does. This hook is an honest degrade: it emits a short notification, not a state-preservation attempt. See scripts/host_adapters.py's HOSTS['cursor']['degrade'] for the declared fallback behavior.

preCompact.command: /usr/bin/env sh -c "echo '{\"user_message\": \"[rig] compaction is happening — Cursor cannot preserve rig run-state through preCompact (observational-only hook); re-anchor to the active task manually if one was in progress.\"}'"
